book_claims.claim: Good source of dietary calcium for vegans when made with calcium sulphate
book_claims.mechanism: Calcium sulphate coagulant provides bioavailable calcium during tofu production

supplementary_research.finding: Prospective study (Ma et al., Circulation 2020) of 210,000+ participants found one or more servings of tofu per week associated with 18% lower coronary heart disease risk.
Calcium from calcium-sulphate-set tofu has bioavailability of approximately 31%, comparable to dairy calcium at 32%, making it an effective non-dairy calcium source (Weaver et al., 2019).
Meta-analysis of 46 RCTs confirmed soy protein (including tofu) reduces LDL cholesterol by 3-4%, with isoflavone-rich forms showing stronger effects (Nachvak et al., 2019).

culinary_pairings.source: Traditional finishing oil for tofu; complementary plant fats
Both provide calcium for plant-based diets
Combined umami; vitamin D from sun-exposed mushrooms aids calcium absorption
Mapo tofu; kimchi jjigae -- classic heat + neutral tofu contrast

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Calcium-sulphate-set tofu has ~31% bioavailability, comparable to dairy at 32%. Check label for coagulant type; nigari (magnesium chloride) set tofu has much less calcium.
Lower than whole soybeans but still pharmacologically significant. Regular consumption supports equol-producing gut bacteria.
Complete protein with all essential amino acids. Firm tofu has more protein per gram than soft/silken varieties.

synergies.description: Both provide calcium for plant-based diets
